advantages: O(1) average membership, insert and delete.
Guarantees uniqueness — duplicates are silently dropped.
Set comprehensions and the constructor make dedupe a one-liner.
disadvantages: Unordered: iteration order is implementation-defined, not insertion order.
Elements must be hashable — no lists/dicts/other sets as elements.
Higher memory overhead than a packed list of the same elements.
Plain `set` is mutable, so it cannot itself be a dict key or set element — use frozenset for that.
